item.title: Chrysler (FCA US, LLC): TIRES:PRESSURE MONITORING AND REGULATING SYSTEMS Recall (2024 jeep wrangler)
item.summary: Chrysler (FCA US, LLC) is recalling certain 2024-2025 Jeep Wrangler vehicles. The remote start antenna cable may be pinched, which can cause the tire pressure monitoring system (TPMS) warning light not to illuminate as intended. As such, these vehicles fail to comply with the requirements of Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standard number 138, "Tire Pressure Monitoring Systems."
item.affectedPeople: A TPMS warning light that fails to notify the driver of a problem increases the risk of a crash.
item.remedy: Dealers will inspect and replace the remote start antenna cable as necessary, free of charge. Owner notification letters were mailed September 2, 2025. Owners may contact FCA US, LLC customer service at 1-800-853-1403. FCA US, LLC's number for this recall is 56C.
item.payoutAmount: Free repair, remedy, software update, or dealer service if stated by the NHTSA recall
